title: Red Hat Security Advisory: ruby:3.3 security update
overview:
An update for the ruby:3.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: ruby:3.3 security update

An update for the ruby:3.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for the ruby:3.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
15dc1615.src.rpm
7018fe7e.src.rpm
faeeed2f.src.rpm
15dc1615.noarch.rpm
7018fe7e.noarch.rpm
faeeed2f.noarch.rpm
15dc1615.i686.rpm
15dc1615.x86_64.rpm
7018fe7e.x86_64.rpm
faeeed2f.x86_64.rpm
15dc1615.s390x.rpm
7018fe7e.s390x.rpm
faeeed2f.s390x.rpm
15dc1615.ppc64le.rpm
7018fe7e.ppc64le.rpm
faeeed2f.ppc64le.rpm
15dc1615.aarch64.rpm
7018fe7e.aarch64.rpm
faeeed2f.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-24294: A denial of service flaw was found in resolv ruby gem. This flaw allows an attacker to craft a malicious DNS packet containing a highly compressed domain name. When the resolv library parses this packet, the name decompression process consumes a large amount of CPU resources, as the library does not limit the resulting length of the name.

CVE-2025-58767: A denial of service flaw has been discovered in the rubygem REXML. Certain input can cause excess cpu usage and given sufficiently large input this can affect program performance.

CVE-2025-61594: A flaw was found in the URI module. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability by using the `+` operator to combine Uniform Resource Identifiers (URIs). This bypasses a previous fix and can lead to the leakage of sensitive information, such as user credentials (passwords), from the original URI, resulting in credential exposure.
